subject: Physical Education
chapter: Physical Education and Sports for CWSN

questions.q: Explain the importance of inclusion of Children with Special Needs (CWSN) in mainstream sports. How can it benefit the society?
questions.answer: Inclusion of CWSN in mainstream sports has numerous benefits for the society. It promotes a sense of equality and acceptance among people. CWSN athletes serve as role models, inspiring others to be more inclusive and accepting. Moreover, their participation in sports promotes social integration and breaks down social barriers.
marking_scheme: Explain the importance of inclusion of CWSN in mainstream sports (2 marks)
Discuss how it benefits the society (3 marks)

questions.q: Describe the role of Para Sports in promoting the inclusion of Children with Special Needs (CWSN) in sports.
questions.answer: Para Sports play a vital role in promoting the inclusion of CWSN in sports. They provide a platform for CWSN athletes to participate in sports, compete with others, and showcase their talent. Para Sports also raise awareness about the abilities and potential of CWSN individuals, challenging stereotypes and promoting a more inclusive society.
marking_scheme: Explain the role of Para Sports in promoting inclusion of CWSN (2 marks)
Discuss the impact of Para Sports on societal attitudes towards CWSN (3 marks)

questions.q: What are the benefits of sports for Children with Special Needs (CWSN)? Discuss the physical, emotional, and social benefits.
questions.answer: Sports provide numerous benefits for CWSN, including physical, emotional, and social benefits. Physically, sports improve coordination, balance, and overall physical fitness. Emotionally, sports boost self-esteem, confidence, and mental well-being. Socially, sports promote socialization, friendship, and a sense of belonging among CWSN individuals.
marking_scheme: Discuss the physical benefits of sports for CWSN (2 marks)
Explain the emotional and social benefits of sports for CWSN (3 marks)

questions.q: A Para-swimmer with a disability can swim a distance of 50 meters in 1 minute and 15 seconds. If she continues to swim at the same speed, how many meters will she swim in 10 minutes?
questions.answer: To calculate the total distance swum, we need to calculate the distance covered in 1 minute and then multiply it by 10. Distance covered in 1 minute = 50 meters. Since she swims for 1 minute and 15 seconds, we need to convert 15 seconds to minutes. 15 seconds = 15/60 = 0.25 minutes. Total time taken to cover 50 meters = 1.25 minutes. Speed = Distance/Time = 50/1.25 = 40 meters/minute. Distance covered in 10 minutes = Speed x Time = 40 x 10 = 400 meters.
marking_scheme: Calculate the speed of the Para-swimmer (2 marks)
Calculate the total distance swum in 10 minutes (3 marks)

questions.q: A Para-athlete with a disability can run 200 meters in 40 seconds. If she continues to run at the same speed, how many seconds will it take her to run 1000 meters?
questions.answer: To calculate the total time taken, we need to calculate the speed first. Speed = Distance/Time = 200/40 = 5 meters/second. Now, we can calculate the time taken to run 1000 meters. Time = Distance/Speed = 1000/5 = 200 seconds.
marking_scheme: Calculate the speed of the Para-athlete (2 marks)
Calculate the total time taken to run 1000 meters (3 marks)

questions.q: A Para-table tennis player with a disability can hit 20 serves in 5 minutes. If she continues to serve at the same speed, how many serves will she hit in 15 minutes?
questions.answer: To calculate the total serves hit, we need to calculate the number of serves hit in 1 minute and then multiply it by 15. Number of serves hit in 1 minute = 20/5 = 4 serves. Total number of serves hit in 15 minutes = 4 x 15 = 60 serves.
marking_scheme: Calculate the number of serves hit in 1 minute (2 marks)
Calculate the total number of serves hit in 15 minutes (3 marks)
